description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a yellow daffodil flower with a long, slender green stem and two elongated, lanceolate leaves. The flower features six broad, pointed petals arranged radially around a central trumpet-shaped corona, which is slightly darker in color. The petals are a soft, pale yellow, while the corona is a deeper yellow with a subtle gradient. The stem is smooth and cylindrical, supporting the flower at an angle, and the leaves emerge from the lower part of the stem, tapering to a point. The overall structure is delicate and natural, with the flower head slightly nodding. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, and landscaped areas.
description: A yellow daffodil flower with six pointed petals, a trumpet-shaped center, and two long green leaves on a slender stem.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved stem, trumpet-shaped corona, and one leaf extending alongside the stem.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the six petals and both leaves extending from the stem.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the corona and petals visible from the side, and both leaves aligned with the stem.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the full face of the flower, six petals radiating around the central corona, and both leaves visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with six distinct petals forming a symmetrical circle around the central corona. The petals slightly overlap at their bases, creating a star-like appearance.
